This is a very well-made instrument with a bold, striking red finish and excellent playability.

Bass Condition:

  • Low, comfortable action
  • Sounds excellent with great dynamic response

Full specifications are visible on the last photo.

This is a cool, rare Japanese bass from the 80s that delivers pro-level performance.

item.title: 【used】momose / MJ1-STD/R 4.15kg #20840【GIB Hyogo】【Plek-adjusted】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $1,835
item.description:
$1,835 · Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

This product has been leveled using PLEK.

The string height at the 12th fret will be 1.5mm for the G string and 2.0mm for the E string (2.3mm for the Low-B string).

Momose, based in Matsumoto, Nagano, is one of Japan's leading brands producing high-quality basses and guitars.

It is renowned for its beautiful appearance, superior sound, and meticulous woodworking techniques.

It enjoys the trust of professionals and is known to be used by top musicians such as Kazuki Isogai and Kyohei Ariga. (Titles omitted)

This MJ1 series is an ambitious work that incorporates Momose's unique design and interpretation into the details, based on the traditional JB style.

The body is made of 2-piece alder, and the fingerboard is made of Indian rosewood—a classic combination.

The bridge is a reliable GOTOH 203B-4, and the tuning machines are also GOTOH GB11WC.

There's no compromise in the selection of hardware.

The pickup is a Momose original VJ-1.

It boasts a rich, sustained mid-low range and a warm, pleasing sound.

It has a deep, versatility that allows it to blend softly yet firmly into an ensemble.

From mellow tones with fingerpicking to driving rock sounds with a pick, its excellent balance shines through, making it a true all-rounder across genres.

Furthermore, the ultra-smooth playability, characteristic of Deviser products, minimizes player stress, which is a huge plus for musicians who require long playing sessions.

This is a guitar I can confidently recommend to anyone looking for a high-quality, standard passive JB that can be taken to any gig.

The frets have approximately 80% remaining.

The truss rod is in good condition.

The neck is in good condition.

The body and paintwork show some scratches, but are in relatively good condition overall.

The electronics are in good condition.
No obvious modifications were observed.

Controls: Neck pickup volume, bridge pickup volume, master tone
Nut width: 34.5mm
Bridge string spacing: 19mm
Scale: 34 inches
Year of manufacture: Unknown
Country of manufacture: JAPAN

During setup, SIT Strings, favored by many professional artists, were used.

They are characterized by stable tuning and a powerful, three-dimensional sound. Please enjoy them.

Delivered after being adjusted for easy playability by GIB craftsmen.

Accessories: Original gig bag

Text: Kaigo Kawami

Inspector: Kawami

Product photos: Muragishi

GIB 3-month warranty included

item.title: 【used】moon / JJ175 EMG 4.86kg #13922【GIB Hyogo】【Plek-adjusted】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $2,588
item.description:
$2,588 · Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

This item has been leveled using PLEK.

The string height at the 12th fret is 1.5mm for the G string and 2.0mm for the E string (2.3mm for the Low-B string).

Moon is a leading, long-established guitar brand in Japan.

Highly regarded by many professional artists, Mitsuru Sudo and Larry Graham are known to be users of this brand. (Titles omitted)

This JJ series, with its slanted front pickup, has been a long-loved flagship model and a signature of Moon.

This particular model is from the JJ series and features an EMG pickup.

It appears to be a JJ-175, but since the wood specifications are not listed in the catalog, it may be a custom model or a made-to-order item.

The body appears to be Flame Tochigi.

The fretboard is Ebony, and the neck is Birdseye Maple.

The striking combination of flame detailing throughout the body and bird'seye finish on the neck creates an exotic and overwhelming presence.

The pickups are EMG.

This specification was also present in the lineup at the time, and is known for its low noise and clear characteristics, synonymous with active pickups.

The controls are simple, featuring neck pickup volume, bridge pickup volume, master tone, and a pickup selector for instant pickup switching.

The sound has a very fast attack, delivering sharp clarity for slap and fingerstyle playing while also outputting a rich, full low end.

It boasts excellent separation, preventing muddiness even during aggressive playing or fast passages, and a clear, defined tone that never gets lost in an ensemble.

It's this sound that has allowed the JJ series to transcend time and remain beloved by top bassists for so long.

The frets have approximately 80% remaining.

The truss rod is stiff, but approximately 90 degrees of movement has been confirmed. The neck is in good condition.
The body and paintwork have scattered small dents and scratches.
The electronics are in good condition.
The capacitor was replaced by GIB due to a tone control issue. The bridge has also been replaced with a GOTOH 404BO-4. The original bridge is included.

Controls: Neck pickup volume, bridge pickup volume, master tone, pickup selector
Nut width: 38.0mm
Bridge string spacing: 19mm
Scale length: 34 inches

During setup, SIT Strings, favored by many professional artists, were used.

item.title: Fender American Vintage II 1966 Jazz Bass RW Lake Placid Blue - 4-String Electric Bass — $2,953.35
item.description:
$2,953.35 · Brand New · MUSIC STORE professional GmbH

  • Bass from the American Vintage II series, made in the USA
  • Authentic replica of a 1966 Jazz
  • Blue nitrocellulose finish with matching headstock and fingerboard binding, four-ply white pickguard
  • Alder body
  • Screw-in maple neck
  • "Round Laminated" rosewood fingerboard with pearloid dot inlays
  • 20 frets in “Vintage Tall” profile
  • "1966 U" neck profile with 38 mm nut width (1.5"), 7.25" fingerboard radius (184 mm)
  • One Fender Pure Vintage ’66 Jazz Bass single-coil pickup in the centre position
  • One Fender Pure Vintage ’66 Jazz Bass single-coil pickup in the bridge position
  • Passive electronics with separate volume pots for each pickup and a treble control
  • Fender Pure Vintage 4-saddle bass bridge with fluted steel string saddles
  • Fender Pure Vintage ‘Lollipop’ bass tuners
  • Third strap button on the headstock (“Hootenanny Strap Button”)
  • Finger rest below the end of the fingerboard
  • Includes a Fender Vintage-Style Black Hardshell Case and bridge cover
A faithful replica of a 1966 Fender Jazz Bass

With the Fender American Vintage II 1966 Jazz Bass RW Lake Placid Blue, Fender is launching an authentic replica of a J-Bass from that vintage, which marked the pinnacle in the evolution of Fender’s arguably most successful bass model. In addition to historically accurate features such as the matching headstock and the bound rosewood fingerboard, Fender has, of course, retained the certified construction of the American Vintage II ’66 Jazz Bass, naturally, the time-honoured construction featuring an offset alder body and a slim maple neck with the narrow ‘1966 U’ profile. Two Pure Vintage ’66 single-coil pickups and the traditional passive electronics also promise a wide range of vintage sounds, making the J-Bass an all-rounder for almost any musical genre. Last but not least, historically accurate hardware in the form of a Pure Vintage 4-saddle bridge and precise Pure Vintage ‘Lollipop’ tuners round off the American Vintage II 1966 Jazz Bass RW Lake Placid Blue from Fender.

Traditional in construction and design

In the construction of the American Vintage II 1966 Jazz Bass, Fender adheres with great attention to detail to the specifications of the vintage year from which it takes its name. Beneath the thin nitrocellulose lacquer lies an alder body, whose ergonomic offset design, with rounded edges and generous comfort contours, ensures the bass guitar is comfortable to play both when slung over the shoulder and resting on the thigh. The bolt-on maple neck naturally features the authentic “1966 U” profile, which, with its pronounced rounding and narrow 38 mm nut width, promises excellent playability for any playing style. The rosewood fingerboard, featuring pearloid dots and elegant binding, is also laminated using the ‘Round Laminated’ method to the 7.25" radius (184 mm) specified by the neck blank.

Authentic vintage sounds for almost any genre

Plugged into a bass amplifier, the American Vintage II Jazz Bass proves to be a tonal all-rounder that not only feels at home in the jazz genre for which it is named, but also offers the right tones for virtually every musical genre, from blues, rock and R&B to funk and metal. Separate volume controls allow for seamless blending of the Fender Pure Vintage ’66 J-Bass single-coil pickups in the bridge and middle positions, providing an extensive range of tones with an unmistakable vintage character, ranging from the wiry, mid-range-focused punch of the bridge pickup to the deep yet transparent sound of the middle pickup. To customise the bass sound, the bass guitar also features the traditional, passive treble control.


Historically accurate hardware specification

The American Vintage II Jazz Bass RW Lake Placid Blue from Fender is completed by historically accurate replicas of its fittings. The roundwound strings, fitted at the factory, are mounted on the body via the traditional Pure Vintage 4-Saddle Bridge, whose fluted steel saddles not only guarantee precise intonation and action adjustment, but also allow the string spacing to be adjusted within certain limits. Behind the bone nut, precise Pure Vintage ‘Lollipop’ tuners with distinctive oval wings also keep the bass securely in tune. Furthermore, the headstock features a third strap button, which on original vintage instruments allowed the strap to be attached to the headstock using the traditional method. Last but not least, the bass comes with a historically accurate bridge cover for optional fitting.

item.title: Ibanez SR5PBLTD Dragon Eye Burst Low Gloss [I200223179] (06/24) — $1,208
item.description:
$1,208 · Good · ISHIBASHI MUSIC

The 2020 limited-edition SR Premium Series, crafted with generous use of exotic woods.

This eye-catching instrument features an African mahogany body combined with poplar burl and birdfs-eye maple for a striking appearance. The low-gloss finish accentuates the three-dimensional depth of the grain, giving it a presence reminiscent of a gDragon Eye,h as the model name suggests.

The neck features an Atlas-5 15-piece construction combining Panga Panga, maple, padauk, purpleheart, and walnut. Reinforced with a KTS titanium rod, it delivers exceptional rigidity and stability. It also boasts a host of features designed to enhance playability, including a roasted birdfs-eye maple fingerboard, stainless steel frets, and Premium fret-edge treatment.

It is equipped with two Nordstrand Big Single pickups. Offering the clear attack and wide dynamic range characteristic of single-coil pickups, they accommodate a wide range of playing styles, from slap to fingerstyle. The combination of the Ibanez Custom Electronics 3-band EQ, active/passive switch, and mid-frequency selector allows for versatile sound shaping.

Despite its 34-inch scale, the slim body shape characteristic of the SR series ensures excellent playability. This model achieves a high level of balance between the powerful low end expected of a 5-string bass and comfortable playability.
The strap pins have been replaced with Fender-style pins.
There are some minor scratches and dents, but no particularly noticeable damage.
There are no noticeable issues with the neck, and it is in good playing condition.
Condition Grade: B+: Some minor scratches, but no issues for normal use

Serial Number: I200223179
Year of Manufacture: 2020
Country of Manufacture: Indonesia
Case: Genuine soft case
Weight: 4.02 kg
Truss Rod: No issues; rod movement tested up to 60 degrees to the left and right with no problems
Remaining Fret Life: Within normal range; fret height is sufficient for playing

Nut width: 44.9 mm

item.title: Ibanez SR924 Black [J80944] (06/24) — $981.91
item.description:
$981.91 · Good · ISHIBASHI MUSIC

An early high-end Soundgear model that fully incorporated the technology Ibanez had developed in the late 1980s.
Made in 1988.
As the flagship model of its era featuring a neck-through construction, this guitar stands out from the later, lightweight, modern SR series with its substantial build and powerful sound. The combination of a maple wing body, a 5-piece neck, and an ebony fingerboard delivers both a rich, full-bodied tone and a crisp attack.

It is equipped with DiMarzio IBZ/USA pickups in a PJ configuration. With the natural response and rich midrange characteristic of a passive setup, it makes its presence felt across a wide range of genres, from rock to fusion. The controls feature a simple layout of Volume, Balancer, and Tone, offering excellent playability during performances.

item.description:
$2,157.61 · Good · ISHIBASHI MUSIC

A custom model based on the 1966 Jazz Bass that incorporates the essence of the early stacked-knob era.

The American Vintage II series is a popular line that thoroughly researches the construction methods and specifications of the era to bring the charm of vintage Fender back to life today. This model features an alder body, a rosewood fingerboard with binding, a 1966 "U"-shaped neck, and Pure Vintage '66 Jazz Bass pickups, delivering the distinctive presence and powerful sound characteristic of mid-1960s Jazz Basses.

This instrument has been modified with stacked knobs for the control section and features CTS 250kƒ¶ pots and paper-in-oil capacitors. Its appeal lies in its appearance, reminiscent of early Jazz Basses, and its vintage-oriented electronics configuration.
Additionally, the bridge has been replaced and the corresponding routing work has been performed.

item.title: XJ-1T 4st -3Tone Burst/Heavy Aged Lacquer-【4.19kg】 — $2,441.85
item.description:
$2,441.85 · Brand New · GUITAR PLANET

Designed and developed in 2009 at Xotic’s workshop in Los Angeles, USA, the XJ-1T series features a 3-band active circuit designed to meet the diverse sound-shaping needs of modern players, while still capturing the tone and playing feel of vintage instruments. This series has earned high praise as a model that fuses tradition with innovation. While adhering to the classic jazz bass style, the series has been refined into a highly polished instrument with practical, ready-to-use specifications, earning deep trust even in professional settings.

The body is crafted from alder, a traditional wood material. It combines moderate midrange projection with a solid low end, ensuring the instrument stands out in an ensemble without getting lost. The bolt-on neck is made of maple, paired with a rosewood fingerboard. This combination delivers a sound with a well-balanced blend of quick attack and supple, warm harmonics. The neck grip is designed with a slightly thicker profile to enhance stability during fingering, reducing fatigue even during extended playing sessions. Furthermore, it has been set up using PLEK technology to optimize fret uniformity and string action balance, ensuring exceptional playability right out of the workshop.

The guitar is equipped with hand-wired pickups from “Raw Vintage,” another Los Angeles-based brand. These meticulously crafted pickups produce a vintage-like response and a tone with depth. The preamp features a well-regarded original circuit. In addition to volume and tone controls, it includes a 3-band EQ (Bass/Mid/Treble), with practical specifications that allow you to switch between different frequency ranges for the mid and treble bands. The ability to instantly fine-tune the sound to suit specific songs or amplifier setups is a significant advantage.

Furthermore, pulling the front pickup’s volume pot switches the instrument to passive mode—a reassuring design that lets you keep playing even if the battery runs out. Pulling the rear pickup’s volume pot switches the two pickups to series wiring. This delivers a thick, warm low end and powerful mid-low frequencies reminiscent of a Precision Bass. One of this instrument’s greatest appeals is its versatility—it covers everything from the clear, open sound of a Jazz Bass to the fat, full-bodied tone of a Precision Bass.

This particular instrument was crafted as a custom order with special specifications. It features the Sunburst finish, widely recognized as a standard color. By incorporating a lightweight Hipshot B-Style bridge, it produces a fat, vintage-feeling sound rich in harmonics—rather than the thin sound often associated with heavier bridges.

Sonically, it precisely emphasizes the midrange characteristic of alder while adding a fat, rich sense of pressure to the low end. By making full use of the active EQ, you can freely control the sound—from subtle nuances to powerful tones. It exudes a solid presence even within a band ensemble, building basslines that powerfully support the entire song.

The XJ-1T uncompromisingly blends a tribute to vintage instruments with modern functionality. Boasting a high level of refinement in appearance, sound, and playability, this model is the perfect choice for players seeking both unique character and practicality.

We invite you to take this opportunity to consider it.


SPEC
Body: Alder
Neck: Maple
Neck Scale: 34"
Fingerboard: Rosewood
Number of Fret: 22,Jescar Silver Nickel(#6150 Type)
Pickups: Raw Vintage JB w/Wood Cover
Preamp: Tri-logic 2 Bass Preamp
Bridge: Hipshot B Style
Control: Neck PU Vol(Push-Active/Pull-Passive),Bridge PU Vol(Push-Parallel/Pull-Series),Tone,Bass,Mid,Treble,Mid SW(400Hz/800Hz),Bright SW(6KHz/12KHz)
Case: Gig Bag
Weight: 4.19kg
Serial: J-3617

Our popular active 5-string bass is now in stock as a used item!!

This is the Yamaha 700 Series BB735A, a bolt-on 5-string bass that carries on the legacy of the legendary BB model.

It features a 3-ply alder/maple/alder body, a 5-piece maple/mahogany neck, a rosewood fingerboard, an 863.6mm scale length, a 600R radius, and a 43mm nut. The pickups are YGD V7 P/J (Alnico V) in a PJ configuration, and it includes volume, balance, 3-band EQ, and an active/passive switch.

item.title: 【used】Sadowsky / Metroline Express RV4E TBT 3.71kg #ME1138【GIB Hyogo】【Plek-adjusted】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $2,589
item.description:
$2,589 · Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

This item has been leveled using PLEK.

The string height at the 12th fret is 1.5mm for the G string and 2.0mm for the E string (2.3mm for the Low-B string).

Sadowsky is a high-end New York brand with top musicians such as Marcus Miller and Will Lee as clients.

This model is from the era when Sadowsky was manufactured in Japan and is now discontinued, but it still enjoys strong support and boasts reliable quality.

It is completely different from the current Metroline Express.

Okada International, which has handled top brands such as Sadowsky NYC and Suhr for many years, was in charge of the basic design, and its high quality is highly regarded.

The body is made of alder, and the fingerboard is made of pau ferro.

The pickups are J-type with a split-coil structure for hum cancellation, as exemplified by DiMarzio.

Because it incorporates Sadowsky's original preamp, the electronics, the heart of the instrument, are on par with those of the original NYC models.

The actual sound is characterized by Sadowsky's signature clear and punchy tone.

It boasts excellent separation, preventing muddiness even when playing chords or fast passages, and maintains a clear, defined tone that cuts through the ensemble.

The noiseless environment unique to hum-canceling pickups and the thick, fast response of the active circuitry are major advantages in live performances and recording.

Furthermore, it offers an extremely smooth playing feel that is immediately apparent, minimizing player stress and offering exceptional playability.

The combination of superior electronics and reliable woodworking techniques makes this a masterpiece worthy of being called the ultimate modern JB.

The frets have approximately 80% remaining.

The truss rod is in good condition.

The neck is in good condition.

There are small dents and scratches on the body and finish, including on the neck, headstock, and body.

The electronics are in good condition.
No obvious modifications were observed.

Controls: Master Volume, Balancer, Master Tone, Treble/Bass
Nut Width: 39.6mm
Bridge String Spacing: 20mm
Scale Length: 34 Inch
Year of Manufacture: Unknown
Country of Manufacture: JAPAN

This guitar will be delivered after being adjusted for ease of playability by a GIB craftsman.

Characterized by a powerful attack and rich harmonics, these strings truly deserve to be called a standard.

These high-quality strings bring out the best in the instrument's performance and character, so please enjoy them as well.

item.description:

This is an exceptional, early-1980s Japanese-made Tokai Jazz Sound, built as a highly precise tribute to a mid-1960s pre-CBS Fender. It is finished in factory gloss Black paired with a red tortoiseshell pickguard, maintaining a completely classic aesthetic.


This early 80’s instrument is in exceptional vintage condition, showing only minimal, normal signs of play wear across the body and neck consistent with its age. The hardware presents beautifully and remains fully functional, while the premium rosewood fingerboard is in pristine condition with absolutely no fretwear. It stands as an incredibly clean, well-preserved, and highly playable example of a golden-era Hamamatsu build.


Brand & Model: Tokai Jazz Sound Bass (JB-Series)

Pickguard: Premium Red Tortoiseshell

item.title: JooDee JRB-55B — Late 1970s Made in Japan — $1,351.45
item.description:
$1,351.45 · Good · Guitar Garage

This is an exceptionally rare, late-1970s Japanese-made JooDee JRB-55B. It stands as one of the most structurally accurate, premium-tier Rickenfakers ever built. Finished in factory "Jetglo" Black with a stark white pickguard, it preserves the classic, aggressive aesthetic of the legendary 4001 blueprint. The JRB-55B was not a cheap visual lookalike, it was engineered using premium materials and advanced structural methods to match the exact physical profile and performance of the American original. 

This vintage JooDee JRB-55B bass is in excellent cosmetic and structural condition, showing very few signs of body wear for its age. The gloss-finished Jetglo body is exceptionally clean, with no notable scuffs, dings, or marks across the front, sides, or back. The chrome hardware remains in great, fully functional condition. Missing the original pickup cover and thumb/finger rest. Fretwork remains great, no heavy pitting. 

  • Era: Circa 1977–1979 (Made in Japan)
  • Construction: Neck-through-body
  • Body Material: Solid Maple
  • Neck Material: Maple 
  • Fretboard: Karin
  • Inlays: Pearloid / Mexican shell triangular "shark-tooth" position markers
  • Scale Length: 33 1/4" (Period-accurate Rickenfaker scale)
  • Pickups: Two JooDee original range single-coils (including the classic chrome bridge assembly bezel)
  • Controls: 2 Volume, 2 Tone knobs, 3-way pickup selector switch
  • Outputs: Dual mono/stereo outputs mimicking Rick-O-Sound wiring
  • Hardware: Vintage chrome open-gear tuners and a heavy-duty adjustable Rickenbacker-style tailpiece
  • Binding: Checkered Binding
  • Pickguard: White single-ply plastic shroud
  • Weight: 3.8kg
  • Case: Gig bag

item.title: MAYONES Jabba HF Aged Blonde 5 [Hadrien Feraud Signature Model] — $5,935.69
item.description:
$5,935.69 · Brand New · Ikebe Music

Our Business Hours is Mon to Fri / 11:00 to 18:00 JPN time
We will close Sat and Sun, so we will reply to inquiry that received weekend on next week.
Your understanding will be appreciated.

We are receiving the customer order with first comes first serves basis both on website and at real shop.
So occasionally it will be sold out while having email communication.
Please understand in advance.

Japanese consumption tax is not required for international shopping. However you may be required to pay duty, tax and import fee when receiving your items in your country. You must pay all these duty, tax and fee as receiver/importer and we do not cover any of them.

■Please note
The product description is an automatic translation of the Japanese description. Please contact us if you have any questions.

-----------------------------------

The latest signature model from Hadrien Feraud, the top French bassist hailed as the reincarnation of Jaco Pastorius, has arrived! This is the first Hadrien Feraud model to feature a lacquer finish and relic treatment!

Since its founding in 1982, MAYONES (also pronounced Mayones overseas) has gained immense popularity not only in Europe and the United States but also domestically, thanks to its unique lineup and reliable quality. Its user list includes a host of top players, such as Hadrien Feraud, Mohini Dey (who was also selected as a tour member for B'z), Nathan Navarro, and Federico Malaman.

The Jabba is based on the JB type, but with a 24-fret design that takes into account balance and playability. The Hadrien Feraud signature model further refines the control placement and neck shape to suit his diverse playing style.
The body is made of swamp ash, the "Aged Blonde" color features a rosewood fingerboard, and the neck is made of maple with TWE processing. TWE (Tonally Enhanced Wood) improves the resonance, stability, and attack of the wood, similar to thermo-roasting or roasting processes.
Furthermore, by applying a lacquer finish and then aging the instrument, it produces a smooth, mellow tone that blends naturally into ensembles, even though it's a brand-new instrument.

The Adrian model features an 18mm string spacing, a neck that is 1mm thinner at the first fret than a standard Jabba, and a narrower neck width around the 12th fret, resulting in exceptional playability for phrasing that dances across the fretboard from the low to high positions, just like Adrian himself. The finger ramp positioned between the pickups has become so iconic that it can now be called his signature feature, and many bass players have installed it on their own instruments.

The built-in Mayones original preamp can be switched between active and passive modes using the push/pull master volume control, allowing for fine-tuning depending on the genre and song. Furthermore, like the latest Jabba models, this unit's preamp can be switched between a 9V battery and a rechargeable battery, and it also features a headphone jack and AUX input, so you can easily practice in hotels while on tour without needing any other equipment.

In 2006, at the young age of 22, he participated in John McLaughlin's album, and the following year, 2007, he released his own solo album and even held an event sponsored by Ikebe Musical Instruments. Since then, he has worked with many artists, and in recent years, he has taken over from Anthony Jackson to participate in the Hiromi Uehara Trio. Please try this instrument endorsed by a world-renowned master musician.

~Comment by Hadrien Feraud~
She's stunning and will simultaneously keep up, play along and challenge you on all aspects and genres. Time to get your act together, that's a keeper right there!

■Body: Swamp Ash Back
■Neck: 1-pc / Hard Rock Maple TEW 34.25
■Fingerboard: Pau Ferro
■Bridge: Mayo Vintage Bass Bridge (18 mm)
■Pickup: JJ / Delano JMVC 5 FE Jazzbass Twincoil Humbucker
■Preamp: Mayo Musashi Nitoryu
■Controls: Volume (Pull/Pass), Balance, Middle, Treble, Bass, Active/Passive mini-switch
■Weight ≈ 4.35 kg

Fender is, of course, the number one brand of electric guitars and basses.

It is loved by countless top artists such as Jaco Pastorius, Marcus Miller, and Geddy Lee. (Titles omitted)

The Jazz Bass was released as a higher-end model than the Precision Bass.

Its sharp and clear sound is loved by many bassists, and it continues to receive overwhelming support and shines brightly even today.

This model is the JB62-75US from the highly acclaimed but now discontinued Fender Japan brand.

As indicated by the US model number, it was originally a higher-end model equipped with US-made pickups as standard.

The body is made of alder, and the fingerboard is rosewood, standard specifications.

A vintage-like spiral bridge is used.

This instrument has had its pickups replaced with Seymour Duncan SJB-3s.

While retaining the authentic Fender feel and basic character expected of a higher-end model, it boasts a more powerful sound.

For those looking for a good, ready-to-use Jazz Bass at a reasonable price, this is a must-try from the Fender Japan era.

The frets have approximately 70% remaining.

The truss rod is stiff, but approximately 90 degrees of movement has been confirmed.

The neck is in good condition.

The body and paintwork show some dents and scratches on the headstock and throughout the body.

The electronics are in good condition.

item.title: Ibanez EHB1505MS -BIF- #I 260304112［3.13kg］［5-string］［Headless bass］［YK012］ 2026 - Black Ice Flat — $1,385.65
item.description:
$1,385.65 · Brand New · G-CLUB Produced by Kurosawa Music

---------------------------------

To customers in Russian Federation,


Due to COVID-19 outbreak, shipping service temporarily terminated from Japan to Russia.

For the above reason, we would like you to do NOT proceed order/transaction.


-------------------------------------------


Welcome to Kurosawa Yokohama! We can ship anywhere all over the world!!


Thank you for looking! Feel free to ask for more details. Also, please carefully read our shop policy below the guitar description!


----------------------------------

The popular 1505! This particular model features a clean, timeless wood grain on the top.


In addition to the portability that is the greatest advantage of the headless style, this bass also boasts ease of playability.


And above all, it's a bass with ingenuity focused on "bass sound." Headless basses have often been criticized for being "not resonant" or "difficult to use," but Ibanez Bass Workshop has re-examined them and created the completely new headless bass, the "EHB."


Ergonomic body design

The EHB body design is ergonomically designed to fit the player perfectly, whether standing or sitting.


The horn is shaped to balance the body and neck when standing and to be easy to hold, while the body curve on the treble string side is designed with particular emphasis on fit and balance when sitting.


The body surface has a contoured edge where the elbow rests. Furthermore, all body edges that come into contact with the body are finished with a smooth radius.


Slanted Body Back

The back of the body is boldly cut to become thinner towards the bass strings. This enhances the fit when seated and is designed with the viewing angle of the fretboard inlays in mind.

In addition, the back of the horn on the treble string side is scooped out to facilitate access to the higher frets.


Chambered Body

The body is chambered to allow for a more intuitive feel of its resonance.

By experiencing the vibrations within the body directly, similar to a hollow body, the player will be able to recognize their own "sound" more clearly.

This also contributes to a slight reduction in weight.


9pc Panga Panga/Walnut Neck with Graphite Reinforcement Rods

A 9pc neck combining Panga Panga wood and walnut is used.

Both are hard woods, making it easy to obtain a clear sound image and characterized by a crisp sound.


Panga Panga Fretboard & Medium Stainless Steel Frets

Features a Panga Panga fretboard, a hard wood that adds a clear core to the sound.


Luminescent Side Dot Inlay

The fretboard features luminescent side dot position markers.


Nordstrand Custom Big Split Pickup

Equipped with a Nordstrand Big Split Custom pickup.


This split pickup offers a sweet tone and rich bottom end. It naturally covers everything from sounds ideal for bass lines to funky slap sounds.


Vari-Mid 3-Band EQ with EQ Bypass Switch

As the abbreviation "Variable frequency control for Mid EQ" suggests, this EQ offers flexible control over the mid-range frequencies.


Typically, a 3-band EQ divides the frequency range into three bands (HIGH/MID/LOW) and allows the developer to control boost or cut by focusing on a frequency range they arbitrarily designate. However, with the Vari-Mid, the user can arbitrarily adjust the Mid focus.


MR5HS mono rail bridge with string spacing adjustable saddles

Features a uniquely designed, newly developed bridge specifically for headless guitars.

Tuning is done by rotating the bridge end. In addition to its unique design, it uses Teflon washers for easy rotation.

The saddles can be moved 1.5mm to the left or right, allowing you to arbitrarily set the string spacing (string pitch) within the movable range. This allows for fine-tuning to suit your playing style.


Newly designed headpieces for EHB

A uniquely designed, newly developed headpiece.

Strings can be easily set and released by turning it from the back with a hex wrench.

It is lightweight, robust, and features a simple design.


Schaller S-Locks strap pins & Neutrik locking jack

Features Schaller S-Locks strap pins for one-touch strap attachment and detachment.

Also features a Neutrik locking jack for secure plug locking and a stable playing environment.


Finger ramp

A finger ramp helps improve playability by enabling a stable form at any position.

The EHB series includes a matte black wooden board that can be used as a finger ramp.

Each EHB series model has a dedicated shape to match the number of strings and scale length.


*Please note that gluing and removing the finger ramp is the customer's responsibility.


SPECIFICATIONS

Neck type: EHB59pc Pangapanga/Walnut neck with Graphite reinforcement rods

Top/Back/Body: Poplar Burl top, Selected lightweight African Mahogany body

Fretboard: Bound Panga Panga fretboard, Off-set Abalone dot inlay

Frets: Medium Stainless Steel frets

Scale: 889-838mm / 35-33"

Number of frets: 24

Bridge: MR5HS bridge

String spacing: 18mm

Pickup: Nordstrand™ Custom Big Split pickup (Passive)

Equalizer: Vari-mid 3-band EQ with EQ bypass switch (passive tone control on treble potentiometer)

Hardware color: Black

Weight: 3.13kg

Accessories: Original gig bag,

If you are looking for that classic, punchy Ernie Ball StingRay tone without breaking the bank, this S.U.B. series is easily one of the best bangs for your buck on the market. It features a solid body, a super comfortable satin-finished maple neck, and a low-noise active humbucking pickup paired with a 9V active preamp (2-band EQ) that lets you dial in everything from deep low-end rumble to crisp, slap-ready highs.

Even though they were built overseas, every Sterling S.U.B. bass was shipped to the official Los Angeles, California headquarters, where a dedicated team of guitar technicians did the final setup and inspection before shipping them out to stores.

item.title: 【used】SCHECTER / PA-MB MASAKI Burned Wood Natural 4.35kg #S2510158【GIB Hyogo】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $1,695
item.description:
$1,695 · Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

SCHECTER is a brand based in the United States that manufactures and sells high-quality guitars.

It boasts a solid history and reputation in the US, having previously handled OEM manufacturing for New York brands such as Sadowsky.

This is the PA-MB, a signature model of MASAKI (CANTA, Jigoku Quartet, DAIDA LAIDA, etc.), one of Japan's leading virtuoso bassists. (Titles omitted)

The body is made of ash and features a Burned Wood Natural finish, where the wood grain is powerfully highlighted by burning it with a torch.

The striking look, which allows you to directly feel the texture of the wood, is very appealing.

The neck is made of 3-piece maple, and the fingerboard is also made of maple.

The joint is a through-neck construction, and the heel is finished very smoothly.

A major feature of this instrument is the inclusion of a Kahler tremolo unit.

This bass guitar allows for dynamic vibrato through whammy bar use and tricky approaches that involve sudden pitch drops, greatly expanding the expressive capabilities of the instrument.

It features two powerful humbucker pickups.

The controls consist of one volume control, a preamp on/off switch, and a rotary pickup selector.

Furthermore, an EQ is integrated into the control cavity, allowing you to pre-set your preferred sound.

To facilitate technical playing techniques such as three-finger picking and tapping, which are MASAKI's specialties, a clear finger ramp is installed between the pickups.

This high level of playability is a major strength.

The sound is characterized by a clear, tight tone with a strong attack.

The sound cuts through well and stands out even in a fierce ensemble.

For bassists who prefer a technical playing style, or players who want to explore innovative approaches, this is a unique and powerful weapon—a truly practical instrument.

The frets have approximately 80% remaining.
The truss rod is in good condition.
The neck is in good condition.
The body and paintwork have no noticeable scratches.
The electronics are in good condition.
No obvious modifications were observed.
Controls: Master volume, preamp bypass switch, rotary pickup selector, (inside the cavity) preamp gain, treble, middle, bass
Nut width: 38.2mm
Bridge string spacing: 19mm
Scale: 34 inches
Year of manufacture: Unknown
Country of manufacture: Japan

During setup, we use our original GIB BASIC / Standard Bass Strings.

These strings are characterized by their straightforward sound and stable playability, naturally bringing out the instrument's inherent character.

These are highly reliable strings that we actually use in our repair and setup workshops.

Please enjoy these strings, which aim to be a new standard for bassists.

GIB craftsmen will adjust the guitar for optimal playability before delivery.

Accessories: Semi-hard case, tremolo arm

Text: Kaigo Kawami

Inspection: Kawami

Product Photos: Terama

item.title: 1976 Fender Precision Bass – Natural Ash / Maple with OHSC — $2,900
item.description:
$2,900 · Very Good · Damon's Surplus Gear

Up for sale is a very nice 1976 Fender Precision Bass in classic original Natural finish on ash body with a one-piece maple “B” neck. This bass features all original electronics and finish. It weighs in at 9.5 lbs, and includes the original hard shell case along with the original manual and cleaning cloth. The only items missing are the chrome pickup ashtray cover and the original thumb rest. I installed an after market thumb rest. Overall condition of the bass is Very Good with only minor scratches, small dings, and light buckle rash consistent with age. The neck is straight and the truss rod turns easily and works as it should. The frets could probably use a level, crown, and polish job but they're in decent and playable shape as is. Just an all-around great-playing and great-sounding vintage P-Bass with excellent collector appeal. But this one deserves to be on a stage.

Feel free to ask questions. Thanks for looking!
